mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.14% is at the effective lower bound, signaling an extended monetary accommodation regime. Sustained low rates compress long-term discount rates and reduce term premiums, causing 10-year Treasury yields to decline as investors seek duration exposure in a low-rate environment.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of 20-day forward periods when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 7.6% signals labor market slack and subdued wage pressure. Persistent slack prevents inflation expectations from rising, allowing the Fed to maintain accommodative policy, which depresses long-term Treasury yields as real rates remain negative.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains above 7.0% for 2 consecutive months
regime_hint: post-shock labor market recovery with slack
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of triggered instances despite elevated unemployment, or correlation between UNRATE and 10Y yield direction falls below 0.15 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 917k units and retail sales at 418.3B dollars show moderate demand. When both demand proxies decline simultaneously, it signals weakening economic momentum, prompting flight-to-safety flows into Treasuries and lower yields.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% month-over-month AND RSAFS declines > 2% month-over-month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 60% of co-triggered instances, or joint decline in HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative 20-day yield moves with Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 99.04 (below trend of 100) indicates supply-side constraint or demand weakness. Sustained sub-trend production signals either stagflationary pressure (if inflation persists) or demand collapse (if inflation falls), both of which compress equity valuations and drive Treasury yields lower as growth expectations decline.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of 5-day forward periods when INDPRO is below 99.5, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 232.77 (monthly index) combined with FEDFUNDS at 0.14% creates a deeply negative real rate environment. If inflation expectations remain anchored while nominal rates stay at zero, real rates compress further, reducing the opportunity cost of holding long-duration Treasuries and pushing yields lower.
regime_hint: low nominal rates with moderate inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction signal fails to outperform single-series FEDFUNDS signal with Rank IC difference < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 135.195M persons combined with unemployment at 7.6% indicates labor market recovery is incomplete. Weak labor demand growth signals subdued wage inflation and limits Fed tightening urgency, keeping long-term rates anchored lower than would occur in a tight labor market.
regime_hint: labor market slack with weak job growth
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 55% of co-triggered instances, or joint signal fails to predict lower yields with Rank IC > 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at 13665.4B (quarterly, lagged 6 months) combined with declining housing starts signals growth deceleration. When quarterly GDP growth slows below 2% annualized and housing permits fall, forward-looking investors reduce growth expectations, causing long-term Treasury yields to fall as terminal rate expectations decline.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth < 0.5% AND HOUST declines > 3% month-over-month
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 60% of co-triggered instances, or interaction signal produces Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 99.04 combined with CPI at 232.77 creates a stagflationary signal: weak supply growth with persistent inflation. In stagflationary regimes, equity risk premiums widen and growth expectations compress, causing long-term Treasury yields to fall despite inflation, as real growth concerns dominate nominal rate expectations.
regime_hint: stagflationary environment with weak supply and sticky inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or stagflation signal produces Rank IC < 0.06 over rolling 2-year windows.
